summary: Good audience (AUD) recording captures a mixed‑quality 1995 show highlighted by an emotional So Many Roads performance.
review: The Schoeps‑mic audience tape is described as very good and immersive, giving the sense of being in the venue. The musical performance is viewed as uneven – Jerry Garcia is said to be off his game, yet the rendition of "So Many Roads" moves listeners emotionally. Overall the show elicits both disappointment and heartfelt appreciation.

ai_rating.rationale: Recording quality is generally praised (good), while show quality receives mixed feedback (some negative remarks about balance, but strong positive reaction to "So Many Roads"). Weighted average yields a mid‑range rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewer notes the tape is "very good" and makes you feel like you're there; no specific dropouts or distortion mentioned. The comment about preferring SBDs suggests this is an audience (AUD) capture.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Performance described as unbalanced without Jerry fully in the mix, but emotional peaks—especially the closing "So Many Roads"—provide memorable moments.

band_member_comments.Jerry: One reviewer says Jerry wasn't firing on all cylinders, yet his playing on "So Many Roads" is praised for its emotional impact.
band_member_comments.Phil: Mentioned playing an Unbroken Chain solo in the spring ’95 double encore.
